title: City Traders Imperium Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: City Traders Imperium Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). City Traders Imperium (3.9★, $97/mo). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tip
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using City Traders Imperium — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is City Traders Imperium?

City Traders Imperium is a South African proprietary trading firm founded in 2018 that provides funded forex and indices trading accounts to qualified traders. The platform offers evaluation programs and direct funding pathways with account sizes ranging from $10K to $200K, along with profit splits up to 80%—competitive rates that make the firm attractive to serious traders. With a 3.9/5 rating on TradingToolsHub and starting prices at just $97/month for the $10K evaluation tier, it's positioned as an accessible entry point into prop trading. What sets City Traders Imperium apart from competitors like FTMO and Topstep is its integrated trading academy with structured mentorship, making it particularly valuable for developing traders who want education alongside capital access.

How to Create Your City Traders Imperium Account

Getting started with City Traders Imperium takes approximately 10-15 minutes. Begin by visiting their official website and clicking the "Join Now" or "Create Account" button. You'll be prompted to enter basic information including your email address, full name, and phone number. City Traders Imperium uses a straightforward verification process designed to comply with South African financial regulations.

Here's what you'll need during setup:

  • Valid email address (Gmail, Outlook, or corporate email)
  • Phone number for SMS verification
  • Government-issued ID (passport or national ID) for KYC verification
  • Proof of residence (utility bill or bank statement dated within 3 months)
  • Trading experience level (they ask this during onboarding)

After submitting your information, you'll receive a verification email within minutes. Click the confirmation link, and your account becomes active immediately. Identity verification typically completes within 24-48 hours. Once approved, you can log in to the dashboard and select your evaluation tier. The entire onboarding process is mobile-friendly, so you can complete it from anywhere.

Setting Up City Traders Imperium for the First Time

When you first log into City Traders Imperium's dashboard, you'll see a clean interface divided into several core sections. On the left sidebar, you'll find navigation for Account, Evaluations, Trading Rules, Education, and Community. The main dashboard displays your account status, balance (if you've funded an evaluation), and quick access to key metrics.

Dashboard Layout: The top banner shows your account tier, available balance, and any active evaluations. Below that, you'll see performance metrics including your current P&L, win rate, and maximum drawdown. The right side includes account notifications and upcoming webinar alerts from their trading academy.

Initial Settings to Configure: First, complete your trader profile under Account Settings. You'll specify your trading style (scalping, swing trading, position trading), your primary markets (forex, indices, or both), and your risk tolerance. This helps the platform tailor recommendations and alerts to your approach. Next, set up two-factor authentication (2FA) for security—City Traders Imperium strongly recommends this given the funded capital involved. Then configure notification preferences: choose whether you want email and SMS alerts for drawdown warnings, trade executions, or weekly summaries. Some traders disable most notifications to avoid emotional reactions; others want real-time drawdown alerts. The choice depends on your trading personality.

Broker Connection: City Traders Imperium provides MetaTrader 4 (MT4) credentials once you've selected an evaluation tier. In your dashboard, you'll find a "Download MT4" button with server details and login credentials. Install MT4 if you don't have it, open the platform, and log in using the provided credentials. Your account should sync within seconds. The platform integrates with their broker partner, so you won't need to manually connect or manage multiple accounts—trades flow directly from MT4 to your City Traders Imperium evaluation account.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Trading Academy & Mentorship City Traders Imperium's most distinctive feature is its integrated education platform. Unlike prop firms that simply provide capital, this platform offers structured courses covering forex fundamentals, technical analysis, risk management, and advanced trading psychology. New members gain access to live mentorship sessions with professional traders—you can book one-on-one calls to discuss your trading plan, get feedback on your strategy, or troubleshoot specific challenges. The academy includes pre-recorded video lessons you can consume at your own pace.

2. Performance Analytics The analytics dashboard breaks down your trading performance with surgical precision. You can view metrics like win rate, average win/loss, Sharpe ratio, and maximum drawdown. The platform displays equity curves, monthly P&L charts, and trade-by-trade analysis showing entry prices, exit prices, and risk-reward ratios. This data is invaluable for identifying patterns in your trading—whether you're stronger at certain times of day, in specific currency pairs, or under particular market conditions. Export your trading history as CSV for further analysis in Excel.

3. Automated Trading & Integration City Traders Imperium supports both manual and automated trading. If you use Expert Advisors (EAs) or trading robots on MT4, they'll run within the platform without modification. The broker integration is seamless: your EA signals execute directly on the funded account. There's no delay or manual intervention required. However, the platform enforces risk rules even for automated trading—your EA can't violate daily loss limits or maximum drawdown thresholds.

4. Risk Management Tools This is non-negotiable for prop trading. City Traders Imperium enforces strict risk parameters: daily loss limits, maximum drawdown limits, and profit targets vary by tier. The dashboard displays a real-time "Risk Meter" showing how close you are to your limits. If your account reaches 95% of the daily loss limit, you'll get an alert. Hit the limit, and trading closes for the day. This forced discipline prevents emotional recovery trading. You can view your rules anytime by clicking "Trading Rules" in the dashboard.

5. Social Features & Community The community section lets you connect with other City Traders Imperium traders. You can post your trading journal, ask questions in forums, share trade ideas, and participate in challenges. The platform occasionally runs trading competitions where top performers win prizes or bonus capital. More importantly, you can learn from other traders' approaches and avoid common mistakes. The community is smaller than massive retail platforms, which means interactions are more meaningful and the mentors actually respond.

6. Flexible Funding Pathways City Traders Imperium isn't rigid—you have options. You can complete a timed evaluation (typically 30 days) with profit targets and drawdown limits. Once passed, you move to a live funded account. Alternatively, if you have previous prop trading experience or verification, you can apply for direct funding, skipping the evaluation step. This flexibility is rare among prop firms and makes it accessible to traders with different backgrounds.

7. Mobile Dashboard While you'll trade on MT4 (desktop), City Traders Imperium's dashboard works on mobile. You can check your P&L, view alerts, and access educational content from your phone. This is useful for monitoring your account between trades or reviewing performance while away from your desk.

City Traders Imperium Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

City Traders Imperium offers four evaluation tiers. Each includes a one-time evaluation fee and monthly subscription cost:

  • $10K Evaluation: $97/month – Entry-level tier for traders starting out. You trade a $10,000 account with smaller profit targets and drawdown limits. Completion time is typically 30 days. Best for traders still building consistent strategies or those wanting to test the platform with minimal commitment.
  • $25K Evaluation: $147/month – Mid-tier option. $25,000 account size with more relaxed rules than the $10K tier. Better profit targets and higher drawdown limits give you more room to trade. Ideal for intermediate traders with proven strategies looking for more capital to scale.
  • $50K Evaluation: $247/month – Advanced tier. $50,000 account allows serious traders to verify larger position sizes. Monthly cost is still reasonable relative to the capital accessed. Suited for traders with 1-2 years of consistent results who are ready for significant capital.
  • $100K Evaluation: $397/month – Professional tier. $100,000 account with the most relaxed drawdown limits. Monthly cost is $397, but you're accessing six-figure capital. Only pursue this if you have a documented profitable track record and can handle the psychological weight of larger positions.

Recommendation by Trader Type:

Beginners (0-6 months experience): Start with the $10K tier at $97/month. Your goal is to prove your strategy works, not to make maximum profit. The lower account size and tighter rules will force discipline. Complete the evaluation, graduate to funded status, and then consider upgrading tiers.

Intermediate Traders (6-24 months experience): If you're consistently profitable on a demo or small real account, jump to the $25K tier ($147/month). You've proven your approach works; now you need capital to scale. The $25K size is the sweet spot between meaningful capital and manageable psychological pressure.

Advanced Traders (2+ years documented profitability): Consider the $50K ($247/month) or $100K ($397/month) tiers directly. If you're already trading profitably, the monthly fee is negligible compared to your potential earnings. The $100K tier is particularly valuable if you have consistent monthly returns exceeding $397—you'll break even on the fee within weeks.

There is no free tier. Every evaluation requires a monthly subscription. However, once you pass an evaluation and become a funded trader, you move to profit-split accounts where you keep up to 80% of your earnings—no further monthly fees.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of City Traders Imperium

1. Use the Mentorship Program Aggressively in Your First Month Your evaluation fee includes access to mentorship calls. Many traders skip this and regret it. Book a mentor session within your first week and show them your trading plan. Ask specifically about whether your strategy aligns with City Traders Imperium's risk rules. Some profitable retail trading strategies actually violate prop firm rules (e.g., holding positions through news events, scaling into losses). Getting this feedback early prevents evaluation failures.

2. Trade the Exact Same Strategy During Your Evaluation as You Plan to Use Afterward Don't test new ideas during your evaluation period. Your goal is to prove consistency, not innovation. Trade the exact system you've backtested and practiced. Once you're funded and generating steady profits, then experiment with variations. City Traders Imperium's analytics tools make this easier to track.

3. Exploit the Community's Profit Target Insights Join the City Traders Imperium community forum immediately. Ask traders who've passed evaluations what profit targets were realistic for them. If someone achieved $1,000 profit on a $25K account within 30 days, that's your benchmark. This inside intel is invaluable and rare among prop firms.

4. Set Alerts for 80% of Your Drawdown Limit, Not the Hard Limit City Traders Imperium closes your account at the maximum drawdown threshold. Don't wait until you hit it. In your notification settings, create custom alerts at 80% of your allowed drawdown. This buffer gives you time to reassess and pause trading before hitting the wall—a critical safety mechanism against emotional spiral trading.

5. Leverage MT4's Backtesting Before You Trade Live Your broker credentials include access to historical data. Use MT4's Strategy Tester to backtest your exact strategy on the pairs and timeframes you'll trade. Run at least 2 years of historical data through your system. This step alone catches fatal flaws—oversights that would kill your evaluation in real-time. City Traders Imperium doesn't restrict this step, so use it.

6. Document Your Trading Plan in Writing Before Day 1 Write down: your target pairs, your timeframes, your entry rules, your profit targets, your stop-loss rules, and your max daily loss threshold. Store this in the City Traders Imperium journal or as a PDF. This document becomes your accountability tool. When you're tempted to deviate during the evaluation, you'll reference it and stay disciplined.

7. Request Direct Funding If You Have Proof of Consistent Profitability If you've been trading forex with real money for 12+ months with verified statements showing profitability, contact City Traders Imperium support and ask about direct funding. Skipping the evaluation tier saves you time and money. They're open to this if your history justifies it.

Common City Traders Imperium Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: MT4 Account Won't Sync or Shows Zero Balance If you've created an evaluation but your MT4 shows zero or an error when logging in, wait 5-10 minutes—the broker takes time to provision accounts. If it persists, check that you're logging into the correct server (City Traders Imperium specifies the server name in your credentials email). If the server is wrong, remove the account from MT4 and manually add the correct server from the dropdown. Contact support via chat if it's still not working; they'll reset your credentials.

Issue 2: Evaluation Rejected for "Account Abuse" or Rule Violations You Didn't Know About City Traders Imperium publishes trading rules, but traders sometimes miss details. Common violations: trading 30 minutes before major news events (not allowed), scaling into losing positions beyond a certain threshold, or holding through economic calendar events. Before starting your evaluation, download the full trading rules PDF from the Rules section and highlight every restriction. If you're rejected and disagree, submit an appeal through the dashboard with specifics of your trading. They're sometimes flexible if you can prove the violation was accidental.

Issue 3: Unsure if Your Strategy Meets Profit Target Requirements The profit targets vary by tier and market conditions. Instead of guessing, use the academy's calculator or ask a mentor. This prevents wasting your evaluation fee on an unrealistic target. A 30-minute mentor call saves you $97 if it prevents a failed evaluation.

Issue 4: Experiencing Drawdown Issues and Worried About Hitting Your Limit If you're approaching your maximum drawdown, the healthiest move is to stop trading for the day and review your strategy. Don't try to "revenge trade" back to breakeven—this is how traders lose funded accounts. Step away, analyze what went wrong, and come back the next day with a clear head. City Traders Imperium's forced stop at the drawdown limit is actually protecting you from yourself.

Is City Traders Imperium Worth It? Our Verdict

City Traders Imperium is worth serious consideration if you're a developing trader seeking structured education alongside capital access. The 3.9/5 rating reflects its strengths (accessible pricing starting at $97/month, integrated mentorship, competitive 80% profit splits) and limitations (smaller than FTMO, limited market diversity beyond forex and indices). Compared to larger competitors, City Traders Imperium is less established, but that also means more personalized attention and community intimacy. We recommend it for: intermediate traders ready to scale beyond demo accounts, traders who value education as much as capital, and anyone building skills in forex and indices specifically. Skip it if you trade equities, cryptos, or options—the platform doesn't support those. For traders seeking alternatives, compare FTMO (larger, more markets) or City Traders Imperium vs FTMO to ensure you're choosing the best fit.
